title: oxalis juruensis var. emarginata
intro: Oxalis juruensis var. emarginata, also known as the Yellow Wood Sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Oxalis juruensis var. emarginata include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and act as a natural diuretic. The plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction for its medicinal uses, and can also be consumed raw in salads or used as a culinary ingredient.
